title: AGENTOBS Event Envelope
description: RFC-0001 §5 — The outermost fixed-structure container applied uniformly to every AGENTOBS-compliant observability event. Targets schema_version '2.0'; implementations MUST also accept '1.0' for backward compatibility (§15.5). Canonical JSON serialisation (§4 P3): keys sorted alphabetically, null values omitted, microsecond-precision timestamps.

schema_version.description: RFC-0001 §5.1, §15.5 — Schema revision. MUST be '2.0' for this specification. Implementations MUST also accept '1.0' for backward compatibility and MUST raise SchemaVersionError on any unrecognised value.

event_id.description: RFC-0001 §6 — Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier (ULID). 26-character Crockford Base32 string; time-ordered with millisecond precision; monotonically incremented within the same millisecond. First character MUST be in range [0-7] (upper 2 bits always zero for 128-bit fit).

event_type.description: RFC-0001 §7 — Namespaced event type. Standard types follow the BNF: 'llm.<namespace>.<entity>.<action>' where namespace is one of the registered built-in namespaces (§7.2). Extension types MUST use reverse-domain prefix outside the llm.* tree (e.g. com.example.entity.action).

timestamp.description: RFC-0001 §5.1, §4 P3 — UTC ISO 8601 timestamp with microsecond precision. MUST be set to now(UTC) if not provided by the caller. Format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.ffffffZ (lower-case time designators as per P3).

source.description: RFC-0001 §5.1 — Emitting component identity in '<name>@<semver>' format. The name component MUST be a valid identifier; the version MUST be a valid Semantic Version 2.0.0 string.

payload.description: RFC-0001 §5.1 — Namespace-specific event data. MUST contain at least one property. The structure is defined by the payload schema for the corresponding event_type namespace (see payloads/ directory). Implementations enforce a maximum nesting depth of 10 (§19.4).

trace_id.description: RFC-0001 §8.1, §14.2 — W3C Trace Context-compatible trace identifier. Exactly 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters encoding a 128-bit trace ID. When present, MUST be identical to payload.trace_id when the payload contains a SpanPayload or AgentStepPayload (§8.1).

span_id.description: RFC-0001 §8.1, §14.2 — W3C Trace Context-compatible span identifier. Exactly 16 lowercase hexadecimal characters encoding a 64-bit span ID. When present and the payload is a SpanPayload, MUST equal payload.span_id.

parent_span_id.description: RFC-0001 §8.1 — Parent span identifier for span tree construction. Same format as span_id. When present with a SpanPayload, MUST equal payload.parent_span_id.

org_id.description: RFC-0001 §5.1, §16.2 — Organisation identifier for multi-tenant isolation. Used by verify_tenant_isolation() compliance checks. All events in a single tenant stream MUST share the same org_id.

team_id.description: RFC-0001 §5.1 — Team within the organisation, for sub-tenant attribution.

actor_id.description: RFC-0001 §5.1 — User identifier or service account that produced the event. Recommended format: '<type>:<identifier>' (e.g. 'user:alice', 'svc:ingest-worker').

session_id.description: RFC-0001 §5.1 — Conversation or request session grouping. Groups all events belonging to a single user session or request lifecycle.

tags.description: RFC-0001 §5.1, §14.1, §19.4 — Flat map of non-empty string keys to non-empty string values. Used for routing, filtering, and OTel attribute mapping (e.g. 'env' maps to deployment.environment.name). Maximum 50 keys.

checksum.description: RFC-0001 §11.2 — SHA-256 digest of the Canonical JSON serialisation of the payload field only. Computed as: 'sha256:' + SHA-256(canonical_payload_json).hexdigest(). Set by sign(). Verifiers MUST recompute and compare using constant-time comparison.

signature.description: RFC-0001 §11.2 — HMAC-SHA256 audit chain signature. Computed as: 'hmac-sha256:' + HMAC-SHA256(event_id + '|' + checksum + '|' + (prev_id or ''), org_secret).hexdigest(). Set by sign(). Verifiers MUST use constant-time comparison (§11.6 item 2, §19.2).

prev_id.description: RFC-0001 §11.3 — ULID of the immediately preceding event in an AuditStream. Enables tamper-evident chaining: any deletion, insertion, or reordering invalidates all subsequent signatures.

then.description: RFC-0001 §11.2 — When checksum is present, signature SHOULD also be present to form a complete signed event.
